items.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
items.summary: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

items.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
items.summary: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

items.title: National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week
items.summary: Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade.

items.title: Trump says ICE agents will assist airport security as DHS shutdown continues
items.summary: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.

items.title: 'We cannot be in peace', say Guthrie family as they renew appeal over missing mother
items.summary: A seven-week nationwide search for 84-year-old Nancy Guthrie, the mother of TV anchor Savannah Guthrie has not identified any suspects.

items.title: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
items.summary: It is illegal for Kenyans to serve in foreign armies and can carry a sentence of up to 10 years in jail.

items.title: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
items.summary: The strike killed 64 people, including 13 children, two nurses and a doctor, according to the WHO head.

items.title: Socialists battle to hold Paris as France holds mayoral elections
items.summary: The capital could shift to the right after 25 years under the Socialists if Rachida Dati defeats Emmanuel Grégoire.

items.title: Russian attacks kill six in Ukraine, officials say
items.summary: The deaths were reported in four south-eastern Ukrainian regions in the past 24 hours.

items.title: Qatari and Turkish citizens die in military helicopter crash
items.summary: Four Qatari military personnel, one Turkish serviceman and two technicians from a Turkish defence company were on board.

items.title: Pop megastars BTS electrify historic centre of Seoul with comeback concert
items.summary: The world's biggest band returns after more than three years with a huge concert in the South Korean capital.

items.title: Robert Mueller, ex-FBI chief who led Trump-Russia investigation, dies at 81
items.summary: A former FBI director, Mueller led the high-profile inquiry into alleged Russian interference in the 2016 US election

items.title: BTS make live return in front of huge crowd
items.summary: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook perform together for the first time since October 2022.

items.title: Trump threatens to send ICE into airports unless funding deal reached
items.summary: The president said ICE would "do security" as airport security staff have gone without pay for weeks due to a partial government shutdown.

items.title: Thousands evacuated as Hawaii faces worst flooding in 20 years
items.summary: "Don't take this storm lightly," Hawaii Governor Josh Green warned on Saturday, as more rain is expected on Oahu and Maui.

items.title: Pentagon restrictions on press violate First Amendment, judge rules
items.summary: The Department of Defense had required reporters to agree to certain rules on what information they could gather in order to maintain access to the Pentagon.

items.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors, jury finds
items.summary: A San Francisco jury concludes Musk’s claims in 2022 were damaging to a group of Twitter investors.

items.title: As Islamophobia rises, Australia's Muslims celebrate Eid
items.summary: Recent events abroad and at home have fuelled an ongoing surge of Islamophobia in Australia.

items.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer actor Nicholas Brendon dies aged 54
items.summary: Brendon died of "natural causes", his family said in a statement, remembering him as "passionate" and "sensitive".

items.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
items.summary: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

items.title: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
items.summary: Kyiv and Budapest disagree on how badly damaged a hub in western Ukraine bombed by Russia really is, as its oil flow remains suspended.

items.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
items.summary: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

items.title: 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict
items.summary: How a lack of justice and trust in the security forces perpetuates deadly violence in Plateau state.

items.title: Italy is voting on whether to change its constitution. What does this mean for Meloni?
items.summary: The dry subject matter of this weekend's vote seems obscure but for many it is a vote on the PM.

items.title: AI videos of sexualised black women removed from TikTok after BBC investigation
items.summary: Dozens of Instagram and TikTok accounts have used AI avatars to promote explicit content, the BBC finds.

items.title: A secularism law some women say makes them feel like 'outsiders' heads to Canada's top court
items.summary: Canada's Supreme Court will hear a challenge to Quebec's Bill 21, which bars some public sector workers, including teachers, from wearing religious symbols.

items.title: 'A direct hit' - BBC visits Israeli town after Iranian strike
items.summary: The BBC's Sebastian Usher reports from Dimona in southern Israel, which was hit by an Iranian missile.

items.title: BBC in Cuba as island plunged into darkness
items.summary: More than 10 million people are enduring power cuts after Cuba's national electrical grid collapsed again.

items.title: Did Australia's under-16s social media ban work?
items.summary: Three months since it came into effect, teenagers tell the BBC how the world-first law has impacted their lives so far.

items.title: 'BTS is everything for us': K-pop fans gather in Seoul for comeback show
items.summary: Some 260,000 fans are expected to watch BTS perform together on Saturday for the first time since 2022.

items.title: Israeli strike next to British journalist is not AI-generated
items.summary: The video of the strike, hitting just metres from RT's Steve Sweeney and his crew, was recorded on Thursday in southern Lebanon.

items.title: I didn't know Epstein was a predator - Norway's crown princess
items.summary: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit has said that she wishes she had never met late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein
